strategic_profile.executive_summary: Brandeis University is a mid-sized, top-tier private research university (R1) located just outside Boston. Founded in 1948 by the American Jewish community on principles of social justice and inclusivity, it offers a rigorous liberal arts education combined with high-level research opportunities. The university is characterized by its collaborative, intellectually curious student body and a test-optional, holistic admissions process.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Early Decision acceptance rates (42.2%) are significantly higher than the Regular Decision rate (~30-34%).
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants with Brandeis as their top choice should strongly consider applying Early Decision to maximize admission chances.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The campus lacks traditional Greek life dominance and major spectator sports, focusing instead on social justice, community service (e.g., the Waltham Group), and academics.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students seeking a typical 'rah-rah' college experience might not be a fit, whereas intellectually curious and activist-leaning students will thrive.

analyst_takeaways.insight: With an R1 classification and a 10:1 student-to-faculty ratio, undergraduates have unusual access to high-level research.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should highlight their research interests and readiness to engage closely with faculty in their application essays.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Brandeis meets 100% of demonstrated financial need and has introduced the 'Brandeis Commitment' to guarantee full or half-tuition for families under specific income thresholds.
analyst_takeaways.implication: It is an highly accessible elite institution for lower- and middle-income students, making it an excellent financial target.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Described as friendly, collaborative, and 'quirky/nerdy in the best way.' Students are deeply engaged in over 200 clubs, cultural organizations, and community service. It is not a party-heavy or sports-centric school.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located on a 235-acre suburban campus in Waltham, 9 miles from Boston. Students rely on university shuttles and the nearby commuter rail to access Boston's vast internship and cultural opportunities. Cars are generally restricted for freshmen and sophomores.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Very High Research Activity (R1). Undergraduates frequently co-author papers, participate in summer SciFest, and secure funding through the centralized Undergraduate Research and Creative Collaborations (URCC) office.

current_status.test_policy_details: Brandeis is fully test-optional. Applicants can decide if their scores accurately reflect their abilities. Submitted scores are considered as part of holistic review.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist acceptance rate jumped significantly in 2024 compared to previous years.

gpa.notes: 33.8% of enrolled students have a 4.0; 31.4% have between 3.75 and 3.99.

testing.policy_note: Only ~30% submit SAT and ~10% submit ACT scores.

colleges.name: School of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students looking for a flexible, interdisciplinary liberal arts education. Students apply generally to the university and declare majors by the end of sophomore year.
colleges.housing_profile: First-years typically live in Massell or North quads; sophomores in East or North; upperclassmen in Ridgewood, Rosenthal, or the Mods.
colleges.student_archetype: Intellectually curious, socially conscious, collaborative, and interdisciplinary.

majors.name: Biology
notable_professors: Hannah Yevick (Mechanics of cell and tissue development)
majors.notes: B.S. requires Physics sequence and extra quantitative/elective courses compared to B.A.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Must pass COSI 12b and 21a to formally declare the major.
majors.notes: COSI 12B is explicitly noted by students as a weed-out class for the major.

majors.name: Economics
majors.special_requirements: Must attain a GPA of at least 2.00 in the 'best' ten Economics courses. Must earn at least C in intro courses and C- in intermediate courses.
notable_professors: Nader Habibi (Economics of the Middle East)
majors.notes: Micro and Macro theory courses are known among students as weeder classes.

majors.name: Business
majors.special_requirements: Must complete ECON 2a or 10a with C- or higher, and BUS 6a and 10a with C or higher to declare.
majors.notes: Offered in conjunction with the Brandeis International Business School.

majors.name: Health: Science, Society, and Policy (HSSP)
majors.notes: A uniquely Brandeis interdisciplinary program bridging science and policy. Very popular for pre-health/pre-med students.

supplemental_requirements.details: 250-word essay addressing the university's founding values of inclusivity and justice. International students must also write a 250-word essay on what excites them about being an international student at Brandeis.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Brandeis places a high value on students who show a genuine commitment to social justice and community service, reflecting its founding principles.

major_selection_tactics: Apply undeclared: All undergraduates enter the School of Arts and Sciences undeclared and have until the end of sophomore year to choose a major, removing pressure to 'game' impacted majors.
college_ranking_tactics: Leverage Early Decision: With an ED acceptance rate of ~42% compared to an overall rate of ~40% (and RD in the low 30s), committing early provides a significant statistical advantage.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Not applicable; Brandeis does not admit by specific major.

scholarships.name: Wien International Scholarship Program
scholarships.benefits: For exceptional international applicants demonstrating strong academic achievement and community involvement.

scholarships.name: The Brandeis Commitment
scholarships.benefits: Guarantees full tuition for families earning $75,000 or less, and half tuition for families earning $200,000 or less with typical assets.

scholarships.name: Tuition Exchange Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: For dependents of individuals who work at participating consortium colleges and universities.

ap_policy.general_rule: A maximum of 16 credits (4 courses) from AP exams may be applied toward the 128 credits required for graduation.
exceptions: Additional exams can be used for placement or to satisfy general degree requirements without granting numeric credit toward graduation.
ap_policy.usage: Credit or Placement depending on department rules.

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards 8 credits for each Higher Level examination with a score of 5 or better, up to a maximum of 16 credits.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students must spend a minimum of two years as full-time matriculated Brandeis students. A maximum of 5 transferred psychology courses count toward the Psych degree. Credit for Israeli university work is strictly limited to a predefined list of public universities.

what_it_takes: Strong alignment with social justice and equity
Evidence of intellectual curiosity and interdisciplinary interests
essay_tips: Directly connect your background to the university's founding Jewish values of inclusion and social justice.
Focus on tangible community impact rather than abstract philosophy.
red_flags: Generic 'Why us' essays that don't reference Brandeis's specific social justice mission.
Expectations of a large state-school sports or Greek life environment.
insights: Students consistently highlight the approachability of professors and the lack of cutthroat competition, favoring collaborative learning.
Housing and dining facilities are frequent points of criticism in student reviews, noted as needing updates.
The 'Brandeis bubble' is real, though shuttles to Boston exist. The campus is known for being quiet on weekends.
